title: sideritis pusilla subsp. briquetiana
intro:
Sideritis pusilla subsp. briquetiana, also known as Briquet's rock tea or mountain tea, is a subspecies of the Sideritis pusilla plant, a type of flowering plant in the mint family, native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean.

This plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sideritis pusilla subsp. briquetiana include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities that can help protect against infections and oxidative stress. The plant is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
